reviewNotes: Thorough, well-structured contract with 28 criteria covering all behaviours specified in product spec §3.10 (Message Flags & Move). Every spec'd behaviour — mark read/unread, flagged/unflagged, move to mailbox, bulk IDs, --account scoping, --dry-run, batched AppleScript — has at least one dedicated criterion with concrete test steps and pass/fail thresholds. Validation edge cases (no flags, contradictory flags, missing --to) are explicitly covered. The contract also correctly adds --json output for both commands, consistent with the project-wide pattern. Safety notes correctly classify mark/move as non-send operations outside the --dangerously-send safety model. Regression sprints [1-6] correctly listed. Out-of-scope items cleanly defer Sprint 8+ features.

criteriaAssessment.testability: All 28 criteria are testable. Each has specific test steps describing exact CLI invocations, mock configurations, and what to inspect in the generated AppleScript. Thresholds are binary (pass/fail) rather than subjective.
criteriaAssessment.completeness: Full coverage of spec §3.10. Mark: all four flags individually (C-161 through C-164), combined flags (C-165), validation (C-166, C-167), bulk (C-168), account scoping (C-169). Move: basic (C-170), account scoping (C-171), validation (C-172), bulk (C-173). Cross-cutting: dry-run for both commands and bulk (C-174 through C-176), human-readable output (C-177), JSON output (C-178), error handling (C-179, C-180), help text (C-181 through C-183), architecture (C-184), test coverage (C-185 through C-187), regression (C-188).
criteriaAssessment.gaps: None identified. All product spec §3.10 key behaviours are covered.

minorObservations.note: Test step says 'ideally one' osascript call, but the threshold says 'batched into one osascript call'. The threshold is stricter — evaluator will hold to the threshold wording. This is fine; just noting the slight inconsistency in language between steps and threshold. Same applies to C-173.

Code inspection criterion is inherently more subjective than the rest (e.g. 'follows the established architecture'). The test steps mitigate this by naming specific functions and modules to look for (build_*_script, handle_mail_error, render_output, run_applescript). Acceptable.

safetyReview: Contract correctly identifies mark and move as non-send operations. The safetyNotes section explicitly states no generated AppleScript should contain a 'send' verb — a good defensive check. The evalTestingSafety note correctly references CLAUDE.md's reversibility requirement. No concerns.
